bonus_fact: The Grand Union Flag had 13 red and white stripes, with the British Union Jack in the corner.

title: Lucky Dozen Hits the Jackpot
story: Cha-ching! On December 3, 1988, twelve lucky players hit the jackpot in the New York Lotto. Their winning numbers (1-8-13-18-28-48) unlocked a whopping $45 million prize. Imagine the excitement as these fortunate folks realized they'd be sharing this life-changing windfall. It was a day when dreams came true for a dozen New Yorkers!
activity_question: What were the winning lottery numbers?
follow_up_question: How do you think the winners felt when they found out they'd won?

title: Rocket-Powered Mail Delivery Takes Off
story: Zoom! On December 3, 2005, a rocket-powered aircraft soared through the sky in Mojave, California. But this wasn't just any flight. XCOR Aerospace made history by delivering US Mail using a manned rocket plane for the first time ever. Imagine your letters zooming to their destination at rocket speed!
activity_question: What did XCOR Aerospace deliver using a manned rocket aircraft?
follow_up_question: Why do you think regular mail isn't usually delivered by rocket?

title: Standing Rock Tribe Wins Pipeline Battle
story: The Standing Rock Sioux Tribe celebrated a major victory on December 3. After months of peaceful protests, the US Army decided not to allow an oil pipeline in North Dakota. The tribe had fought hard to protect their sacred lands and water sources. In 2016, their voices were finally heard, showing the power of standing up for what's right.
activity_question: What did the US Army decide not to allow?
follow_up_question: Why do you think protecting water sources was important to the Standing Rock Sioux Tribe?

title: Breaking Barriers in the Courtroom
story: Edith Sampson made history in 1962. On December 3, she became the first Black woman to serve as a judge in Illinois. Sampson's appointment broke down barriers in the legal world. Her achievement opened doors for others and showed that anyone could reach great heights with determination. Sampson's story inspires us to dream big and work hard to make positive changes in our communities.
activity_question: What position did Edith Sampson achieve in 1962?
activity_choices: First Black woman judge in Illinois
First Black woman mayor of Illinois
First woman to argue before the Supreme Court
activity_answer: First Black woman judge in Illinois
follow_up_question: Why do you think it's important to have diverse representation in the justice system?

title: The Artist Behind Washington's Famous Portrait
story: Brush strokes of destiny! Gilbert Stuart, the painter who would create America's most iconic portrait, took his first breath in 1755. Born in Saunderstown, Rhode Island on December 3, Stuart grew up to become a master artist. His unfinished painting of George Washington is so famous, you probably see it every day - it's the image on the one-dollar bill!
activity_question: What famous portrait did Gilbert Stuart create?
activity_choices: An unfinished portrait of George Washington
A painting of the Statue of Liberty
activity_answer: An unfinished portrait of George Washington
follow_up_question: Can you think of other everyday items that feature famous artwork?
